# ARC API by SourceFuse

> ARC API is an open-source Rapid Application Development framework built on LoopBack 4 for developing cloud-native enterprise microservices. It provides 16 pre-built, production-ready microservices with multi-tenant support, standardized authentication, and deployment flexibility across private and public clouds.

## Overview

- License: MIT
- Framework: LoopBack 4 (TypeScript/Node.js), NestJS compatible
- Monorepo: Lerna + npm Workspaces (independent versioning)
- Documentation: https://sourcefuse.github.io/arc-docs/arc-api-docs
- Repository: https://github.com/sourcefuse/loopback4-microservice-catalog
- CLI: `npm install -g @sourceloop/cli`

## Core Packages

- @sourceloop/core: Foundation framework — models, components, sequences, decorators, mixins, multi-tenancy, authentication/authorization utilities
- @sourceloop/cli: Scaffolding CLI for backend (LoopBack 4), Angular, and React apps with MCP integration
- @sourceloop/cache: Redis-based caching extension with repository mixin and decorators
- @sourceloop/feature-toggle: Feature flag extension with method-level decorator support
- @sourceloop/file-utils: File upload utilities with S3 storage and ClamAV virus scanning
- @sourceloop/ocr-parser: Angular module for OCR-based document clause extraction

## Microservices

### Authentication & Identity
- @sourceloop/authentication-service: Multi-tenant auth with OAuth (Google, Apple, Facebook, Keycloak, Azure AD, Auth0), SAML, MFA, JWT management
- @sourceloop/oidc-service: OpenID Connect identity provider (DEPRECATED)
- @sourceloop/user-tenant-service: User, tenant, team, role, and permission management with RBAC

### Communication
- @sourceloop/notification-service: Multi-channel notifications — email (SES/Nodemailer), SMS (SNS), push (PubNub/Socket.io)
- @sourceloop/in-mail-service: Internal mail system with threads, attachments, and bulk operations
- @sourceloop/chat-service: Real-time messaging with individual/group chat and file attachments
- @sourceloop/video-conferencing-service: Video conferencing via Vonage and Twilio with archive support

### Workflow & Tasks
- @sourceloop/bpmn-service: BPMN workflow management with Camunda engine
- @sourceloop/task-service: Event-driven task management with Kafka/SQS/HTTP connectors
- @sourceloop/scheduler-service: Calendar and event scheduling with attendees and working hours

### Data & Analytics
- @sourceloop/audit-service: Audit logging with S3 archival and Excel export
- @sourceloop/reporting-service: Dashboard and widget-based reporting with S3 export
- @sourceloop/search-service: Full-text search across multiple models (PostgreSQL/MySQL)
- @sourceloop/survey-service: Survey creation with question types, sections, cycles, and response tracking

### Commerce
- @sourceloop/payment-service: Payment processing via PayPal, Stripe, and Razorpay
- @sourceloop/feature-toggle-service: Feature flag management at system, tenant, and user levels

## Quick Start

```bash
# Install the CLI
npm install -g @sourceloop/cli

# Scaffold a new project
sourceloop scaffold

# Create a microservice
sourceloop microservice

# Run database migrations
npm run db:migrate
```

## Common Environment Variables

All services share these base environment variables:

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| NODE_ENV | Environment (production/development) |
| LOG_LEVEL | Logging level (info/debug/error) |
| DB_HOST | Database hostname |
| DB_PORT | Database port |
| DB_USER | Database username |
| DB_PASSWORD | Database password |
| DB_DATABASE | Database name |
| DB_SCHEMA | Database schema |
| REDIS_HOST | Redis hostname |
| REDIS_PORT | Redis port |
| REDIS_URL | Redis connection URL |
| REDIS_PASSWORD | Redis password |
| JWT_SECRET | JWT signing secret |
| JWT_ISSUER | JWT token issuer |

## Database Support

- Primary: PostgreSQL
- Secondary: MySQL (survey-service)
- ORM: LoopBack 4 Repository pattern with optional Sequelize support
- Migrations: db-migrate with per-service environment variable controls
- Soft Delete: Built-in via loopback4-soft-delete mixin

## Migration Environment Variables

Each service uses a dedicated environment variable to control migrations:

| Service | Migration Variable |
|---------|-------------------|
| Authentication | AUTH_MIGRATION |
| Audit | AUDIT_MIGRATION |
| BPMN | WORKFLOW_MIGRATION |
| Chat | CHAT_MIGRATION |
| Feature Toggle | FEATURETOGGLE_MIGRATION |
| In-Mail | INMAIL_MIGRATION |
| Notification | NOTIF_MIGRATION |
| OIDC | OIDC_MIGRATION |
| Payment | PAYMENT_MIGRATION |
| Reporting | REPORTS_MIGRATION |
| Scheduler | SCHEDULER_MIGRATION |
| Search | SEARCH_MIGRATION |
| Survey | SURVEY_MIGRATION |
| User-Tenant | AUTH_MIGRATION |

## Deployment

- Docker: Dockerfiles provided for all services and sandbox examples
- Docker Compose: Full-stack compose file in `/sandbox/docker-compose.yml`
- AWS CDK: CDK deployment stacks with migration support
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL + Redis minimum; Camunda for BPMN; Vonage/Twilio for video

## Links

- Main Documentation: https://sourcefuse.github.io/arc-docs/arc-api-docs
- Website: https://www.sourcefuse.com/arc-by-sourcefuse/
- GitHub: https://github.com/sourcefuse/loopback4-microservice-catalog
- Contributing: https://github.com/sourcefuse/loopback4-microservice-catalog/blob/master/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md
